topic.what: Network meta-analysis comparing the efficacy of 14 different H. pylori eradication regimens, including standard triple therapy, sequential therapy, concomitant therapy, hybrid therapy, and bismuth-based quadruple therapy.
topic.how: Bayesian network meta-analysis integrating direct and indirect evidence from RCTs to rank all regimens by eradication rate.
topic.why_matters: Demonstrates that concomitant, hybrid, and sequential therapies consistently outperform standard triple therapy, providing evidence-based guidance for first-line regimen selection in an era of rising antibiotic resistance.

characteristics.what_it_measures: Comparative eradication efficacy across 14 H. pylori treatment regimens
characteristics.vs_baseline: Standard triple therapy ranked significantly lower than concomitant, hybrid, and sequential regimens
characteristics.evidence: Network meta-analysis of multiple RCTs with Bayesian ranking probabilities
characteristics.context: Global data; local resistance patterns may shift optimal regimen choice

good_for: Selecting first-line H. pylori eradication regimens
Guideline development for empiric H. pylori treatment
Understanding comparative efficacy across regimen classes
not_for: Regions where local antibiotic resistance data already dictates regimen choice
Individual patient-level treatment decisions without considering local resistance
requires: Knowledge of local clarithromycin and metronidazole resistance rates
Access to the component antibiotics of recommended regimens

novelty: First comprehensive network meta-analysis to simultaneously rank 14 H. pylori regimens
Provides probability-based ranking rather than simple pairwise comparisons
open_problems: Incorporation of susceptibility-guided therapy into the network comparison
Updating the analysis with post-2015 trial data reflecting further resistance evolution
Optimal regimen selection for rescue therapy after first-line failure
